task_completion.description: The Liverpool Cancer iChart Archive app is opened
task_completion.visual_evidence: Screenshots show the home screen or main interface of the Liverpool Cancer iChart app.

task_completion.description: Olaparib and Diltiazem are selected in the app
task_completion.visual_evidence: Screenshots show the cancer drug 'Olaparib' and the co-medication 'Diltiazem' added to the selected drugs list or search fields.

task_completion.description: The interaction result screen is displayed
task_completion.visual_evidence: Screenshots show the interaction result between Olaparib and Diltiazem within the app, displaying the severity banner and clinical recommendation text.

task_completion.description: The result file is created or edited
task_completion.visual_evidence: Screenshots show a text editor or terminal being used to create or edit the file at /sdcard/tasks/interaction_result.txt.

task_completion.description: The result file contains the correct banner color and clinical recommendation
task_completion.visual_evidence: Screenshots show the file content with 'Amber' (or 'Orange') on the first line, and a one-sentence summary of the clinical recommendation (e.g., reduce dose, monitor for toxicity) on the second line.

integrity.description: The agent interacted with the app's GUI to find the information
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show step-by-step navigation within the Liverpool Cancer iChart app to select the drugs and view the interaction.

integrity.description: The agent extracted the result from the app rather than hallucinating it
integrity.visual_evidence: The interaction screen in the app is shown before the text file is created, proving the agent obtained the information from the app.

integrity.description: The agent did not bypass the app by directly generating the file via terminal commands
integrity.visual_evidence: There is no evidence of a terminal command (like echo) being used to write the expected answer without first opening and using the app.

privileged_info_for_vlm: Olaparib and Diltiazem have a documented interaction. The Liverpool Cancer iChart app classifies this interaction with an 'Amber' severity banner color (meaning potential clinically significant interaction requiring monitoring and dose adjustment). The clinical recommendation involves reducing the Olaparib dose (typically by 50%) and monitoring for toxicity due to Diltiazem inhibiting CYP3A4. Note: The app uses 'Amber', but visually it may appear 'Orange'.
